title: potentilla crantzii var. hirta
intro: Potentilla crantzii var. hirta, also known as hairy tormentil, is a perennial plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions. The health benefits of hairy tormentil include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Hairy tormentil can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
